title: taenitis interrupta
intro: Taenitis interrupta, also known as Interrupted Taenitis, is a species of fern native to New Zealand. It has been used in traditional Maori medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing pain, fever, and inflammation, as well as acting as an antiseptic. In herbal preparations, Taenitis interrupta is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and poultices, which are applied topically to treat skin conditions and wounds.
